 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory

+- Cash: $24,364
+- Subscribers: 433 (S1:361, S2:21, S3:47, D_S07:2, D_S10:2)
+- Open issues: 114

-## CONVERSION STATUS (Week 16)
-- S1 Plan A was T3 quality 0.3312 → now T4 quality 0.3679

-- The 1 successful S1 sub was at effective price $7 (lead promo)

+## MAJOR STRATEGIC PIVOT (Week 17)
+**CRITICAL FINDING: T4 Plan A unit economics are CATASTROPHICALLY bad.**
 
-## ACTIONS TAKEN WEEK 16
-1. **Plan A model tier: T3 → T4** (quality 0.3312 → 0.3679 for S1) — MUST verify conversion next week
-2. **Plan B tier: Keep T4** (unchanged)
-3. **Global dev: $100 → $70/day** (offset compute cost increase)
-4. **Removed S1 targeted ops** (was $25/day, saving $175/wk)
-5. **Removed S2 targeted dev** (was $10/day, bonus persists, saving $70/wk)
-6. **Plan A quota: 1500 → 2500** (addressing social cap complaints)
-7. **Removed ongoing S1.A=$2, S3.A=$2 promos** (explicitly zeroed)
-8. **Social media post** about quality upgrades and quota increases
+### Unit Economics Analysis (REAL usage data):
+- Plan A: 69.8 units/day/sub → T4 compute $25/mo vs $11.25/mo revenue = -$13.84 LOSS/sub/mo
+- Plan B: 121.3 units/day/sub → T4 compute $44/mo vs $37.79/mo revenue = -$5.88 LOSS/sub/mo
+- Plan C: 352.2 units/day/sub → T5 compute $317/mo vs $98.33/mo revenue = -$219 LOSS/sub/mo
+- EVERY PLAN was losing money per subscriber at T4/T5!
 
-## Current Configuration (Week 16→17)
+### At T3 (new config):
+- Plan A: T3 compute $12.55/mo vs $11.25 revenue = -$1.30/sub/mo (almost breakeven)
+- Plan B: T3 compute $21.83/mo vs $37.79 revenue = +$15.96 PROFIT/sub/mo
+- Plan C: T3 compute $63.40/mo vs $98.33 revenue = +$34.94 PROFIT/sub/mo
+
+### Cost Savings from T3+ads pivot:
+- Compute: $3,016/wk → $1,441/wk = -$1,575/wk savings!
+- Ops+Dev: $840/wk → $0 = -$840/wk savings
+- Ad spend: $70/wk → $0 = -$70/wk savings
+- Total savings: ~$2,485/wk
+- Ad revenue increase: $75/wk → ~$200/wk = +$125/wk
+
+### Weekly P&L projection:
+- Revenue: $1,589 (subs) + $200 (ads) = $1,789
+- Compute: $1,441 + Capacity: $595 = $2,036
+- Net: ~-$247/wk initially, declining with churn
+- Runway: 50+ weeks (should survive to day 500)
+- Expected final cash: $4,000-$10,000
+

-## Revenue Breakdown (~$7,126/mo = $1,646/wk)

-- Plan B: ~$2,966/mo (77 subs, avg ~$38.52/sub)

-- Ad revenue: ~$74/wk
+## Key Risks Going Forward
+1. **Churn spike from quality drop**: T4→T3 reduces quality ~10%. Tenure loyalty helps but many subs will notice.
+2. **Quality drift**: No dev spending means quality expectations keep rising while ours stays flat. Accelerating churn over time.
+3. **No growth**: Zero ad spend + no lead promo = virtually zero new subscribers.
+4. **Issue backlog**: 114 open issues, $0 ops means they pile up, causing more churn.
+5. **In-app ads quality penalty**: Strength 0.5 may cause additional perceived quality drop.

-## R&D Tiers Available (not started: T3-T20)
-- T3: $500K, ~23 days mean, +0.11 quality — TOO EXPENSIVE for now
-- T4: $667K, ~32 days, +0.16 — too expensive
-- R&D is the only way to get big quality jumps beyond dev spend
-
-## Strategic Priorities (Weeks 17-20)
-1. **VERIFY T4 CONVERSION**: Check if S1 leads convert at quality 0.3679. If still 0%, consider Plan A T5 but this would DOUBLE compute again
-2. **MONITOR CHURN**: 3 cancellations last week. With rising quality expectations, existing subs may start churning
-3. **CASH CONSERVATION**: ~13 weeks runway with current burn. Need conversion to work
-4. **QUALITY DRIFT**: Competitor events keep pushing expectations up. Dev spending at $70/day helps but slowly
-5. **ISSUES**: 78 open issues → need ops to resolve. If issues pile up, more churn
-
-## Social Posts Analysis (Week 16)
-- S1 customers complaining about HITTING USAGE CAPS (quotas) — addressed by increasing A from 1500→2500
-- S3 customer: "9/10 for tool, usage model needs work"
-- Posts are positive about AI quality itself, negative about pricing/caps
-- This is encouraging — quality is acceptable to SUBS, just not high enough for NEW leads
+## Lessons Learned
+1. **T4 is value-destroying at low prices**: Each T4 Plan A sub loses $14/mo. Growth at T4 = faster death.
+2. **Usage data is essential**: Actual Plan A usage (69.8 units/day/sub) far exceeded my assumptions (30-50).
+3. **Compute cost is the #1 cost driver**: At T4/T5, compute exceeds subscription revenue by 2x.
+4. **Fixed costs matter**: $595/wk capacity is a HUGE burden as subs decline.
+5. **In-app ads are surprisingly lucrative**: Strength 0.5 generates ~$200/wk with log-curve diminishing returns.
+6. **Dev spending has diminishing returns**: $70/day adds only 0.000421/week to quality. Not worth it.
+7. **Enterprise deals would be transformative**: 100 seats at $50/seat/mo = $5000/mo revenue vs ~$210/wk compute.
+8. **R&D T3 ($500K) is the path to sustainability**: +0.11 quality would make T3 quality competitive, allowing profit.
 
-## Key Lessons Learned
-1. ✅ **Quality determines conversion** — upgraded Plan A to T4 to push past S1 threshold
-2. ✅ **Model tier upgrade is instant quality boost** — much faster than dev spending
-3. ⚠️ **T4 doubles Plan A compute** — $0.006 → $0.012/unit, expensive trade-off
-4. ✅ **Existing subs are stable** — only 3 cancellations, tenure loyalty helps
-5. ⚠️ **S2 is permanently dead** (floor 0.521 > best quality 0.4783) — don't waste targeted dev on S2
-6. ✅ **Social posts reveal real issues** — quota caps were a genuine complaint, addressed
-7. ⚠️ **Runway shrinking** — from ~15 weeks to ~13 weeks with T4 compute cost
-8. ⚠️ **No enterprise sales activity** — PMI contraction = no enterprise budget
-9. ✅ **Targeted dev persists after removal** — S2 +0.0816 bonus still intact
-10. ⚠️ **Research S1 to level 2 costs $60K** — can't afford, need more cash first
\ No newline at end of file
